sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, nutrient payoff is the whole point.
sections.subtitleText: 0.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, nutrient payoff is the whole point.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 9.6g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 9.6g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 0.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: 0.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin a at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 11% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin a at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 11% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: very strong vitamin A identity. this is a major category strength and part of what makes carrots a genuinely good vegetable. cheap and practical. easy to buy, store, snack on, and cook with. easy raw-snack use. a practical advantage compared with fussier vegetables.

displayItems.title: very strong vitamin A identity
displayItems.explanation: This is a major category strength and part of what makes carrots a genuinely good vegetable.

displayItems.title: cheap and practical
displayItems.explanation: Easy to buy, store, snack on, and cook with.

displayItems.title: easy raw-snack use
displayItems.explanation: A practical advantage compared with fussier vegetables.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: very strong vitamin A identity. this is a major category strength and part of what makes carrots a genuinely good vegetable. cheap and practical. easy to buy, store, snack on, and cook with. easy raw-snack use. a practical advantage compared with fussier vegetables.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: sweetness is a bit higher than the cleanest vegetables. still fine, just not ultra-low-sugar by category standards. micronutrient spread is narrower than elite greens. great at some things, less absurdly dense overall than the very top vegetables. protein is basically irrelevant. not a real flaw, but still nearly absent.

closing.summary: Carrots does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
